text.title: Ruby ActiveRecord Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for idiomatic ActiveRecord usage.

rule.statement: WHERE clauses used in ≥2 call sites MUST be extracted to a named scope on the model. Inline `Model.where(...)` duplication is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Scopes give business meaning + centralize change; duplication drifts.

rule.statement: Collections that will iterate over associations MUST use `includes`/`preload`/`eager_load` to load them up front.
rule.rationale: Lazy access in iteration triggers N+1; one of the top performance regressions in Rails apps.

rule.statement: Any sequence with ≥2 writes (save/create/update/destroy) MUST run inside an `ActiveRecord::Base.transaction do ... end` block. `update_all`/`delete_all` for state-changing business logic is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a transaction, mid-call failures leave partial state; update_all skips callbacks + validations.

rule.statement: Columns representing a closed set of states MUST use `enum status: { pending: 0, paid: 1, ... }`. Magic strings/integers in queries are forbidden.
rule.rationale: enum gives scope methods, predicate methods, validation, and prevents typo bugs.

rule.statement: Code MUST use `Time.current` / `Date.current` (timezone-aware). `Time.now` / `Date.today` (server timezone) is forbidden in business logic.
rule.rationale: Time.now uses server timezone; switching servers or using AR queries with stored timestamps produces off-by-N-hour bugs.
